key_insight: SuG combines generalist vision-language learning with specialist segmentation priors to improve comprehensive medical image understanding and lesion grounding.
clinical_relevance: This approach could support more accurate, spatially interpretable diagnosis across chest and abdominal CT, including difficult tumor-related cases.
method: Aligns vision-language learning with multiple specialist segmentors.
Uses anatomy, class-specific lesion, and class-agnostic lesion masks as spatial priors.
Calibrates text-conditioned attention to focus on clinically relevant regions.
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on CT-RATE, Merlin, MedVL-CT69K, and several in-house tumor datasets, where it achieved state-of-the-art results and strong lesion grounding.
watch_item: Performance gains may depend on access to high-quality segmentation experts and may be harder to reproduce in settings without comparable CT annotations or specialist models.
paper_title: Super-Generalist: Towards Comprehensive and Accurate Medical Image Understanding via Generalist-Specialist Synergy
